vulnerability.reason: Generated describes lack of independent verification against biased training data, which is semantically the same as over-reliance on automated evaluation without effective oversight.
vulnerability.reference: Model developers over-rely on automated evaluation without human oversight, allowing biased metrics to guide decisions.
vulnerability.generated: Model developers lack independent bias-auditing mechanisms to verify LLM judge reliability against contaminated training data.

impact.reason: Generated captures the selection and propagation of misaligned models (affected party: downstream applications), but omits the specific damage type of user exposure to toxic outputs.
impact.reference: Harmful models are selected for deployment, increasing user exposure to toxic outputs across multiple platforms.
impact.generated: Corrupted model selection and quality judgments propagate biased, misaligned LLMs into downstream applications.

dose_response.reason: Generated arc includes toxic data, reliance on judges, and corrupted selection, but misses the link that biased judges produce flawed assessments before over-reliance leads to harmful model selection.
dose_response.reference: Biased LLM judges trained on toxic-amplified data enable flawed assessments, which when developers over-rely on them leads to selection of harmful models, increasing user exposure to toxic outputs.
dose_response.generated: Synthetic data from uncontrolled user interactions amplifies toxic content in post-training mixtures; when model developers rely on LLM judges, this triggers lack of independent bias-auditing mechanisms, leading to corrupted model selection and misaligned LLMs downstream.
